name: wsdiscovery
description: WS-Discovery protocol scanner for discovering and enumerating ONVIF cameras and IoT devices on the network. Use when you need to discover ONVIF devices, cameras, or WS-Discovery enabled equipment on a network.
capabilities: Guide users through WS-Discovery protocol scanning
Help configure target hostname or IP address for scanning
Display discovered device information (IP, ports, device types, manufacturer details)
Support verbose mode for full XML response viewing
Guide network broadcast scanning for comprehensive device discovery
author: BrownFineSecurity
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill that provides instructions for using the external wsdiscovery CLI tool. No executable code, no network calls, no filesystem access. The static analyzer produced false positives by misinterpreting JSON metadata and markdown documentation as code patterns. All reported findings are false positives from documentation, not actual security risks.
valueStatement: Finding network devices like ONVIF cameras requires understanding the WS-Discovery protocol. This skill provides guidance for discovering and enumerating WS-Discovery enabled equipment on your network using the wsdiscovery tool.
userTitle: Discover ONVIF cameras and IoT devices
limitations: The wsdiscovery binary must be installed separately on the system
Only discovers devices that support the WS-Discovery protocol
Cannot scan networks without proper authorization
Does not perform vulnerability testing or credential exploitation

useCases.title: Manage network device inventory
useCases.description: Discover and catalog ONVIF cameras and IoT devices across your managed network infrastructure

useCases.title: Conduct authorized device surveys
useCases.description: Perform device discovery as part of authorized security assessments and penetration testing

useCases.title: Find devices for integration
useCases.description: Locate ONVIF cameras and devices for building video management integrations and applications

promptTemplates.title: Basic discovery scan
promptTemplates.scenario: Scan a specific host for devices
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me discover WS-Discovery devices on host 192.168.1.100 using wsdiscovery

promptTemplates.title: Full XML output
promptTemplates.prompt: Run wsdiscovery with verbose mode on 192.168.1.100 to see full XML responses

promptTemplates.title: JSON format export
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan 192.168.1.100 and output device information in JSON format

promptTemplates.title: Network broadcast scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan the broadcast address 239.255.255.250 to find all WS-Discovery devices on the network

bestPractices: Obtain proper authorization before scanning any network you do not own
Use the quiet format option when you only need device counts or summary information
Combine with onvifscan skill for comprehensive device testing after discovery
antiPatterns: Assuming all discovered devices are secure or trustworthy
Using discovered device endpoints without proper security assessment

faq.question: What is WS-Discovery protocol?
faq.answer: WS-Discovery is a network protocol used by ONVIF cameras, printers, and IoT devices to advertise their services on a network using multicast/broadcast messages.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, the wsdiscovery command-line tool must be installed separately. This skill provides instructions for using it once installed.

faq.question: What devices will this find?
faq.answer: Only devices that support the WS-Discovery protocol. This includes most ONVIF cameras, network video recorders, and some printers and IoT devices.

faq.question: Is network scanning legal?
faq.answer: Scanning networks you do not own or have permission to test may be illegal. Always obtain proper authorization before scanning.

faq.question: How is this different from onvifscan?
faq.answer: wsdiscovery discovers devices advertising on the network. onvifscan tests discovered devices for ONVIF compatibility and vulnerability testing.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Three formats are supported: text (default, human-readable with colors), JSON (machine-readable), and quiet (minimal output).
